elements.sent-2411-004: Motivated by problems from complexity theory, geometric functional analysis, and computer science, numerous remarkable results have been developed in the hypercube framework, making Boolean analysis one of the most active areas in discrete Fourier analysis, combinatorial optimization, and related fields in the past decades.
elements.sent-2411-005: To state results, we begin with recalling basic concepts and notions in hypercube setting.
elements.sent-2411-006: For fixed $n\in\mathbb{N}$, let $\{-1,1\}^n$ be the hypercube equipped with the uniform probability measure $\mu_n$, and let $L_{p (\{-1,1\}^{n )$ be the associated $L_{p}$ space for $1\leq p\leq \infty$.}}
elements.sent-2411-007: In the sequel, we will use the shorthand notation $[n]\coloneqq\{1,2,\cdots,n\}$.
elements.sent-2411-008: For each $j\in[n]$, the $j$-th influence of $f:\{-1,1\}^n\to\mathbb{R}$ is given by \begin{equation}Inf_j(f)=\mu_n(\{x\in \{-1,1\}^n|\, f(x)\neq f(x^{\oplus j})\}),\end{equation} where $x^{\oplus j}$ means flipping the $j$-th variable of $x$, i.e. for $x=(x_1,\cdots, x_n)\in \{-1,1\}^n$, \begin{equation}x^{\oplus j}=(x_1,\cdots,x_{j-1},-x_j,x_{j+1},\cdots,x_n).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-009: The total influence of $f$ is defined by $\mathrm{Inf (f)=\sum_{j\in[n]}\mathrm{Inf _j(f)$, which is often used to measure the complexity of the function $f$.}}
elements.sent-2411-010: To illustrate the analytic property of the $j$-th influence of $f$, we now recall the $j$-th partial derivative of the $f:\{-1,1\}^{n}\to \mathbb{R}$ as follows: \begin{equation}\dc_{j}(f)(x)\coloneqq\frac{f(x)-f(x^{\oplus j})}{2},\quad x\in\{-1,1\}^{n}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-011: In particular, for each Boolean function $f:\{-1,1\}^n\to\{-1,1\}$, it is easy to compute that \begin{equation}\label{Inf-Dj} \mathrm{Inf}_j(f)=\|\dc_{j}(f)\|_{L_p(\{-1,1\}^n)}^p,\quad \forall 1\leq p< \infty, j\in[n].\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-012: Bounding the (total) influence of $f$ in terms of the variance of $f$ is one of the essential themes of Boolean analysis, which is closely related to specific types of functional inequalities on the hypercube.
elements.sent-2411-013: In particular, the Poincar\'e inequality on the hypercube can be stated as follows (see e.g. \cite[p.\,36]{ODo2014 ): \begin{equation}\label{Poincare inequality} \var(f):=\|f-\mathbb{E}_{\mu_{n}}(f)\|_{L_2(\{-1,1\}^n)}^2\leq \sum_{j=1}^{n}\|\dc_{j}(f)\|^{2}_{L_{2}(\{-1,1\}^{n})},\end{equation} where $\mathbb{E _{\mu_{n (f)$ is referred as the expectation of $f$ with respect to the uniform measure $\mu_{n}$.}}}}

elements.sent-2411-381: For each $j\in J$, by Lemma \ref{lem Tj 1 , we have \begin{equation}\sum_{\substack{\supp(\s)\subseteq J^{c} |\supp(\s)|=d-1,\alpha\in \{1,2,3\}}}|\widehat{T}(\s\oplus e^{\alpha}_{j})|^{2} =\left(\tr\left[\overline{T}_{j}\cdot A_j\widetilde{T}_j\right]\right)^{2} =\left(\tr\left[E_{J^c\cup\{n+1\}}(\overline{T}_{j}\cdot A_j\widetilde{T}_j)\right]\right)^{2} =\left(\tr\left[\overline{T}_{j}E_{J^c\cup\{n+1\}}( A_j\widetilde{T}_j)\right]\right)^{2},\end{equation} where we used that each conditional expectation preserves trace and $\mathcal{E _{J^c\cup\{n+1\} (T_{j )=T_j$ (this follows from the definition of $T_j$ as in \eqref{eq:Tj ).}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-382: Using Lemma \ref{lemmaIntT we have \begin{equation}\left(\tr\left[\overline{T}_{j}E_{J^c\cup\{n+1\}}( A_j\widetilde{T}_j)\right]\right)^{2} \leq \left(\tr\left[|\overline{T}_{j}|\cdot|E_{J^c\cup\{n+1\}}( A_j\widetilde{T}_j)|\right]\right)^{2} =\left\{\int_{0}^{\infty}\tr\left[\mathds{1}_{(t,\infty)}(\left|\overline{T}_{j}\right|)\cdot|E_{J^c\cup\{n+1\}}( A_j\widetilde{T}_j)|\right]~dt\right\}^{2} \leq 2\left\{\int_{0}^{t_{0}}\tr\left[\mathds{1}_{(t,\infty)}(\left|\overline{T}_{j}\right|)\cdot|E_{J^c\cup\{n+1\}}( A_j\widetilde{T}_j)|\right]~dt\right\}^{2} \quad+2\left\{\int_{t_{0}}^{\infty}\tr\left[\mathds{1}_{(t,\infty)}(\left|\overline{T}_{j}\right|)\cdot|E_{J^c\cup\{n+1\}}( A_j\widetilde{T}_j)|\right]~dt\right\}^{2} \coloneqq 2Y_{1,j}(T)^2+2Y_{2,j}(T)^2,\end{equation} where $t_0>0$ is chosen to satisfy \begin{equation}\label{specific choice} \exp\left\{-\frac{d\cdot t_{0}^{2/d}}{2e}\right\}=\sum_{j\in J}\|\dc_{j}(T)\|^{2}_{L_{1}}=M_J(T).\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-383: Note here that \eqref{specific choice and the assumption $M(T)\leq e^{-2d}$ imply that \begin{equation}t^{2}_{0}=\left(\frac{2e}{d}\right)^{d}\left(\log\left(\frac{1}{M_J(T)}\right)\right)^{d}\quadand\quad t_{0}\geq (4e)^{\frac{d}{2}}.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-384: We conclude from the above argument that \begin{equation}\label{lhs key lem} \sum_{j\in J} \sum_{\substack{\supp(\s)\subseteq J^{c} |\supp(\s)|=d-1,\alpha\in \{1,2,3\}}}|\widehat{T}(\s\oplus e^{\alpha}_{j})|^{2}\leq 2\sum_{j\in J}\mathsf{Y}_{1,j}(T)^2+2\sum_{j\in J}\mathsf{Y}_{2,j}(T)^2.\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-385: By Lemma \ref{Y1j , we have \begin{equation}\sum_{j\in J}Y_{1,j}(T)^2\leq t_{0}^2\sum_{j\in J}\left\|\dc_{j}(T)\right\|_{L_{1}}^2=\left(\frac{2e}{d}\right)^{d}M_J(T)\left(\log\left(\frac{1}{M_J(T)}\right)\right)^{d}.\end{equation}.}
